111229.explanation: In the program, GbB explicitly chooses referenda for impactful decisions (such as those about wind turbines or asylum reception locations), with a feasible threshold and serious follow-up of the result.

111233.explanation: GbB is explicitly in favor of energy cooperatives with at least 75% local ownership. Financial support for collective energy projects is part of the policy.

111234.explanation: GbB allows room for economic development through a 'yes, unless' mentality, but sets conditions (landscape integration, traffic, environment). The context determines the choice.

111235.explanation: GbB recognizes the Dispersal Act and legal obligations. We propose doing this on a small scale (maximum 8/16 people per location) with proper integration, but no asylum seekers center.

111236.explanation: GbB sees entrepreneurs as partners. Taking care of their own housing aligns with employers' responsibility and less government burden, but we do not want to make it mandatory on their own premises.

111238.explanation: GbB chooses practical, feasible measures. No ban on wood burning, but stimulation of cleaner alternatives fits the 'yes, unless' mentality.

111244.explanation: GBB states 'participation must remain possible for everyone' and focuses on affordable membership fees by making accommodations more sustainable. A football club falls under this, but an (expensive) fitness club does not.

111245.explanation: We consider a good basic provision of information necessary. It must be at a sufficiently professional level. The local broadcaster is one of the means to oversee politics.

111248.explanation: GBB views sports clubs as important for social cohesion. Affordable accommodations and membership fees are key points. We try everything to keep facilities available in villages and neighborhoods.

111249.explanation: GbB considers monument care important for preserving the landscape and cultural heritage. This fits the 'yes, unless' mentality.

111252.explanation: Currently, GbB sees no real signals why this would be useful. If necessary, a camera can be placed temporarily first to ascertain the necessity.

111253.explanation: GbB supports social safety and assistance with violence. This fits within the 'care structure with rapid help.' But 'must' is a strong word; this also depends on the care sector.

111259.explanation: GbB prefers a 'work first' approach and likes to support that, but also stands against unnecessary bureaucracy. Counterperformance can be useful but must primarily contribute to sustainable engagement.

111260.explanation: GbB focuses on combating poverty and providing assistance. Exemption from fees is a form of social policy that GbB supports. Additionally, we focus on addressing poverty in general so that the need for support decreases.

111263.explanation: GbB supports sustainable mobility. This aligns with the energy transition and ambitions for cleaner transport.

111264.explanation: GbB focuses on traffic safety, safe crossings, and school environments. 30 km/h contributes to the safety of vulnerable groups, but in many places that is already implemented. GbB would prefer to invest in street design that automatically reduces speeding where desired.

111265.explanation: GBB respects cultural diversity and inclusion. This aligns with personal freedom of choice. Naturally, within the same reimbursement conditions as for other residents.

111267.explanation: GbB chooses affordability for residents. An increase in property tax (OZB) hampers the housing market and homeowners. An OZB increase is, in our view, a last resort if it really cannot be found elsewhere.

111269.explanation: GbB supports tourism but examines expenditures. Lowering tourist tax reduces municipal income.

111271.explanation: GbB focuses on livability in village centers but wants space for entrepreneurs. This is a balance between economy and peace, and above all a decision made by the village entrepreneurs together.

111274.explanation: GbB sees opportunities in the energy transition with agricultural businesses as partners. Solar panels can make land multifunctional.

111276.explanation: GbB puts 'villages with character' at the center. Preservation of regional dialect is part of this. We would certainly support it if both the Achterhoeks dialect and culture receive attention.

111277.explanation: GbB focuses on safety and livability. Coffeeshops may not fit here, but we want to look at the overall picture with any request, where a legal chain is conditional, including cultivation.

111282.explanation: GbB focuses on flexible housing. This helps create housing supply faster and offers flexibility.

111284.explanation: GbB is explicitly against large wind turbines of 150 meters or higher, the only party that has not changed its opinion on this. It was the reason for the coalition breakup. We are in favor of small turbines of 25 meters. We also focus on village energy where residents and businesses jointly generate energy with the municipality.

111285.explanation: GbB sees energy innovations in farmers as an opportunity. Manure digestion aligns with sustainable agriculture and the energy transition, but we want to add that we are not in favor of large-scale manure digestion, where it is very likely that manure is collected from outside (manure pit).

111229.explanation: We have a representative democracy (elections). In the formation/implementation of policy, we do not exclude consulting a representative group of residents (general interest).

111233.explanation: We believe in the importance of local collaboration in the generation of sustainable energy and meeting our own energy needs. This requires local support, local ownership, and the development of local energy systems.

111234.explanation: The infrastructure (physical and digital) is not suitable for establishing large distribution centers.

111235.explanation: Bronckhorst takes its responsibility by implementing the Dispersal Act. We make agreements with society to handle this properly.

111236.explanation: The housing of seasonal workers must take place in a proper and humane manner. This does not have to be on the employer’s own company site. The primary responsibility lies with the employer or the dispatching organization.

111238.explanation: We strive for responsible wood burning. Good communication is part of this.

111244.explanation: CDA stands for an inclusive society. Everyone participates; no one is left behind.

111245.explanation: We continue the current contribution - no increase for now - because we support the importance of the regional broadcaster. This does not exclude that, due to new insights, we may come to a different consideration.

111248.explanation: Together with sports clubs, we invest in strong associations and a healthy society. This includes a good offer within accessible distance.

111249.explanation: Bronckhorst values the monuments, religious heritage, and historic estates. We support their preservation with knowledge and expertise.

111252.explanation: The municipality currently has no locations where camera surveillance is necessary. However, if reasons arise related to public order and safety, we will not exclude it, with great attention to privacy.

111253.explanation: The availability of a shelter for victims of domestic violence is important in Bronckhorst or the immediate surroundings.

111259.explanation: We implement social assistance legislation and preferably help people towards regular employment. Where social work contributes to this, we support it. Our goal is for people to feel valued and able to participate.

111260.explanation: We want to prevent people from ending up in a prolonged financially uncertain situation and thus having to rely on our financial support for a long time.

111263.explanation: We ensure that the right facilities are realized in the right place at the right time.

111264.explanation: Where reducing speed contributes to safety, this will be applied.

111265.explanation: We provide appropriate care so that people can live independently for a long time and children can develop in a safe environment. For this, we deploy professional support and exclude no one.

111267.explanation: Municipal finances are under pressure, and therefore we make choices in existing and new policies. We do not exclude an increase in property tax. We continue to strive for the lowest possible tax burden for our residents. We consider this in relation to comparable municipalities.

111269.explanation: Tourist tax remains at an acceptable level and is also used to enhance attractiveness for tourists.

111274.explanation: We operate on the principle that good agricultural land remains available for family farms. We do not exclude exceptions at the edges of industrial areas and on remediated agricultural buildings. Good landscape integration is a precondition.

111276.explanation: Speaking Low Saxon is part of the Achterhoek culture, our traditions, and customs. This contributes to participation and a livable society. We want to discuss this with schools.

111277.explanation: This does not address a demand and certainly does not contribute to the health of a society we stand for.

111282.explanation: We want to allow temporary housing where necessary to alleviate acute housing shortages, but permanent housing prevails.

111284.explanation: We do not rule out wind turbines. They must be integrated carefully, with attention to limiting nuisance and respect for the landscape. Revenues should also stay within the area. Currently, Eldrik is the only search area designated by the council.

111285.explanation: Manure digestion contributes to the energy transition and local energy issues. Manure as a raw material for green gas is an option.

111229.explanation: We find it important that residents can have a say and think along. There are ways other than a referendum.

111233.explanation: Especially for residents who want to become more sustainable but cannot afford it. This also applies to groups of residents who want to generate energy together, such as an energy cooperative.

111234.explanation: Distribution centers pollute the landscape and hardly provide local and permanent jobs. Often this work is done by migrant workers under poor working conditions.

111235.explanation: We take our responsibility. Small-scale shelter fits in Bronckhorst municipality, provided it is well embedded. We listen to local concerns and involve the neighborhood well in the plans. Connecting with newcomers is important.

111236.explanation: Living on an industrial estate is not good. Separating living and working is healthy and reduces the risk of exploitation.

111238.explanation: Wood burning is bad for health. We want to gradually reduce wood burning. We pay attention to residents who burn wood because they cannot pay the gas bill.

111244.explanation: Currently, residents with an income up to 130% of social assistance already receive this. That is very good and must remain so.

111245.explanation: Local media are extremely important. They bring regional news from the street or the municipal council. It is the national government's role to determine the financial contribution.

111248.explanation: Playing sports is healthy, gives a lot of energy, and satisfaction. The sports club is also a meeting place. Yet we are cautious with financial contributions from the municipality. First, we look at what is going on and then assess what is needed.

111249.explanation: Our monuments make our municipality unique, beautiful, and attractive to residents and tourists. We believe you must take good care of them. It would be a great shame if unique places are lost forever because we now choose to cut back.

111252.explanation: The feeling of safety is important. More cameras in Bronckhorst are not the solution, as privacy is a great good. We prefer more enforcement officers or temporary camera surveillance in places where it proves necessary.

111253.explanation: There are too few shelter places, both nationally and in the Achterhoek. If you are no longer safe at home, there must be a place in the region you can always go to.

111259.explanation: Meaningful work can contribute to a sense of self-worth. We find participating in society important, provided it is voluntary, suits the person, and does not displace regular work.

111260.explanation: Waiver of municipal taxes is sometimes necessary so that people on low incomes do not get into further trouble. GroenLinks-PvdA wants every resident to receive help so basic expenses can be paid and debts do not increase further. We want to maintain the generous threshold of 130%.

111263.explanation: Sufficient charging points are needed so that electric driving is accessible, the energy transition can accelerate, and sustainable mobility can become the norm. As a large rural municipality, we must ensure a good spread of charging points.

111264.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports a maximum speed of 30 km/h in built-up areas. A lower maximum speed improves traffic safety in our villages. For cycling children and elderly people with limited mobility, this is a world of difference. It is also cleaner for the environment.

111265.explanation: Freedom of choice in healthcare is a right for everyone, but due to shortages in care, choosing is not always possible. We find equality, quality, and accessibility of healthcare very important.

111267.explanation: For GroenLinks-PvdA, an increase in property tax is negotiable if it is necessary to improve facilities such as schools, libraries, or sports. We keep an eye on homeowners on the poverty line.

111269.explanation: Tourists make full use of facilities, such as our beautiful walking and cycling paths. Maintaining these costs money.

111271.explanation: We prefer to keep the agreements as they are. We find it important that shops can open on Sundays for shopkeepers and customers who want it. This also makes it much easier for single people who work during the week to do important shopping.

111274.explanation: A field of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want to, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, all kinds of things can still live and bloom under solar panels, such as sheep and flowers.

111276.explanation: The language is a living part of Achterhoek culture, but the pressure on classroom time is already high in education. In our view, Achterhoeks should have a place in locations such as the library, the sports club, and the local theater.

111277.explanation: Regulating is better than prohibiting; a coffeeshop can reduce illegal trade and increase control.

111282.explanation: Temporary housing can be a temporary solution to reduce pressure on the housing market.

111284.explanation: Wind turbines are necessary for the energy transition. We cannot manage with only solar and saving energy. We have designated the area around Eldrik for wind turbines. GroenLinks-PvdA stands by this.

111285.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA agrees with small-scale manure digesters for personal use. We do not support large-scale manure digesters.

111229.explanation: "Selective" use in case of particularly significant subjects.

111233.explanation: Investing is primarily the responsibility of the residents themselves. The municipality can occasionally support collective initiatives financially, for example at the start.

111234.explanation: Large storage/distribution centers can/may establish themselves on regional business parks in the Achterhoek. Bronckhorst's focus is mainly on local employment.

111235.explanation: A large-scale asylum seekers' center does not fit with Bronckhorst. The choice is for more small-scale refugee reception based on strict conditions, and in accordance with the obligations of the Distribution Act and agreements of the Achterhoek Region.

111236.explanation: The principle is good, namely that employers are responsible for good housing. This may not always be possible on their own property, then possibly elsewhere under strict conditions.

111244.explanation: Bronckhorst already has generous financial arrangements for people with low incomes, including costs for sports clubs.

111245.explanation: The importance of local media is obvious to the VVD. However, the current financial support from the municipality of Bronckhorst is good. Additional funding is not strictly necessary.

111248.explanation: Occasional financial contributions are possible, for example during the corona period and energy cost developments. Structurally, the association itself is responsible for sound operations.

111252.explanation: More cameras are not a goal in themselves. Only apply them where necessary for safety.

111253.explanation: Ensure a well-organized women's shelter in the larger Achterhoek Region. Doetinchem has a central role.

111259.explanation: Strengthens social contacts/bonds with the local community.

111260.explanation: Bronckhorst has a debt waiver policy. It is good to help people out of debt on a structural basis.

111263.explanation: Anticipate well on the growth of electric cars.

111264.explanation: Already applies to various village centers and neighborhoods. The current situation is good for now. In the future, perhaps selectively expand. Traffic safety is decisive.

111265.explanation: Good care should be the starting point. Regardless of faith, culture, or belief.

111267.explanation: Tax increases should be limited as much as possible. A maximum of inflation adjustment.

111271.explanation: Safeguard entrepreneurial freedom. Often combined with beautiful local initiatives and events on Sundays.

111274.explanation: Allow selectively (small-scale) to help achieve regional/local energy transition goals.

111276.explanation: Some specific attention to regional culture/regional language is fine.

111277.explanation: Coffeeshops primarily in Doetinchem as the central municipality/city. Not allowed in small villages like those in Bronckhorst.

111282.explanation: Temporary housing is okay to address housing shortages. Permanent housing construction remains a priority.

111284.explanation: Bronckhorst is co-responsible for contributing to the Regional Energy Strategy (RES) of the Achterhoek Region. Wind turbines only possible in the search area Eldrik under strict conditions (health/nature/environment) and (financial) local participation.

111229.explanation: Councillors are elected by the residents. They are the people's representatives on behalf of our residents. In the future, an advisory referendum can be established. For this, it is necessary to clearly define what an important subject is that affects all residents in Bronckhorst.

111233.explanation: As a municipality, we must do everything possible to implement the energy transition as quickly as possible. That is good for the wallets of our residents. Gas will become increasingly expensive. Electricity is the future. Energy from, for, and by Bronckhorst ensures that the benefits remain local.

111234.explanation: There are no real distribution centers yet in Bronckhorst. D66 Bronckhorst can imagine that this will be needed on a small scale in the future. Goods in the future will not only be bought in physical stores but also delivered from small-scale distribution centers to homes.

111235.explanation: There will be no asylum seekers' center in Bronckhorst. Therefore, this statement is not relevant. However, there will be reception for Unaccompanied Minor Refugees. These are not asylum seekers' centers. Across the Achterhoek, each municipality takes a part in a regional deal of the objectives set in the Distribution Act.

111236.explanation: Agricultural and horticultural companies must provide well-equipped housing for seasonal workers; the so-called labor migrants. This does not necessarily have to be on the company's own premises. It is healthy to be able to distance oneself from work during rest periods.

111238.explanation: The use of fireplaces and wood stoves is part of our DNA in the Achterhoek. However, we need to monitor better whether residents comply with the 'red' code for wood burning. When this code is active, the released fine particles cannot dissipate. That can seriously affect others (for example, people with asthma).

111244.explanation: We stand for a municipality where everyone has the opportunity to participate. People who still have a bit of month left at the end of their money should receive help to make themselves and their children part of (sports) clubs. That could also be a music association.

111245.explanation: A vital local democracy depends on an independent local/regional broadcaster. Their journalists are the 'gadflies' of politics and municipal policy. We want to cherish that now that local commercial media has seriously declined. Besides, they are also carriers of culture.

111248.explanation: Do not automatically support, says D66 Bronckhorst. First conduct a thorough analysis of why a shortage arises. The time is past when every association can hold out their hand for a few extra cents. Simply put, the local budget is no longer high enough for that.

111249.explanation: We must cherish our monuments. Bronckhorst has a great many monuments because we are such a large municipality in terms of land area. We do not receive a national contribution based on this. That contribution is based on 36,000 inhabitants. So we cannot avoid making choices.

111252.explanation: Camera surveillance is an effective tool to increase the feeling of safety. Our police and public order officers (BOA's) cannot be everywhere at once. However, clear agreements must be made about how long footage may be stored and for what purpose.

111253.explanation: We first need to assess whether there is enough demand for Bronckhorst to have its own 'stay-away-from-my-house' service, or if this should be developed across the entire Achterhoek region. For D66 Bronckhorst, there is no question that there must be a safe shelter for anyone who feels threatened. That must happen.

111259.explanation: We wish everyone receiving social assistance to have a voluntary social role. That brings joy to life. But the reality is that this is not possible for everyone. Residents do not end up on social assistance without reason. Often there is something going on. Tailored solutions are needed.

111260.explanation: Municipal taxes should indeed be waived for residents with a very low income. If society refuses to bear that, it causes even more misery. People run into financial trouble. In recent years, we have seen what that can lead to. Neighborly support is then needed.

111263.explanation: The number of electric cars will increase rapidly in the coming years. A car is indispensable in the Achterhoek. Good infrastructure, including sufficient charging points, is a must for the modern municipality we want to be.

111264.explanation: Where possible, speed limits should be 30 km/h, but not everywhere. Where separate bike lanes run alongside roads for cars, this is not necessary for traffic safety, according to experts. It can even become more dangerous. We prefer the province to maintain speed limits of 60-80 km/h on its N roads.

111265.explanation: Everyone is free to choose the health insurance that suits them best. The government should not interfere in this, unless it negotiates collective affordable health insurance for specific groups who otherwise would have no access to healthcare.

111267.explanation: The property tax (OZB) here averages €938, whereas in the Netherlands it is €1001. Bronckhorst is a relatively affluent municipality. Many households will not eat less because of an OZB increase. An increase is necessary now that national funds for Bronckhorst will decrease and we aim to maintain facilities.

111269.explanation: The tourist tax must be increased. Many horeca entrepreneurs will confirm that raising it will not reduce visitors. Compared to other attractions, Bronckhorst charges a low tourist tax, while tourists make use of our extensive infrastructure.

111271.explanation: Without Sunday shopping days, there is no 'buzz' in our village centers. Therefore, Sunday shopping must remain. That is part of a municipality where the leisure economy-read tourism sector-plays an increasingly important economic role in Bronckhorst.

111274.explanation: We must move towards large-scale production of fossil-free energy, not only for the environment but also to keep energy affordable. This requires solar parks and a limited number of wind turbines. Energy from, for, and by Bronckhorst ensures that part of the profit remains here.

111276.explanation: The municipality does not decide on the content of educational materials used in schools. If schools choose to pay attention to the Achterhoeks dialect or culture, that is their own choice. We can only encourage it.

111277.explanation: As far as D66 Bronckhorst knows, this issue is not currently relevant. No application has been submitted. Hence our choice of 'neither.' If a request does come in the future, D66 Bronckhorst will approach it with a positive attitude provided the BIBOB Act is complied with (or everything is completely legal). Research has shown that alcohol use is more dangerous for public health than smoking a single joint.

111282.explanation: Temporary housing can provide relief in our housing market in coming years. This allows especially first-time buyers to find affordable housing faster. Nowadays temporary homes have become beautiful homes where you might not want to leave anymore :-).

111284.explanation: On December 18, 2024, the municipal council decided by a large majority. A limited number of wind turbines will be built near Eldrik. D66 Bronckhorst does not want to reverse this decision. That would be improper governance. Meanwhile, the first initiators have already come forward.

111285.explanation: In Steenderen, there is now a plan for a manure digester as part of the Steenderen energy roundabout. We will first await the outcomes of this plan. D66 Bronckhorst is not in favor of every farmer building a manure digester. That does not fit in the environmental vision.

111229.explanation: Referendums are only a useful instrument in cases of municipal mergers and the like.

111233.explanation: However, the municipality should help such initiatives as much as possible.

111234.explanation: Large companies should be located on industrial sites such as the A18.

111235.explanation: There are already AMV facilities (special youth care locations) in our municipality. With this, we comply with the distribution law.

111236.explanation: Employers should take responsibility for their employees.

111238.explanation: Providing information and guidelines is useful.

111244.explanation: Tailored solutions are very important here. Look at what is best for each person.

111245.explanation: Given the financial situation, we think it is unwise to do this. It could also jeopardize independence.

111248.explanation: Creativity and entrepreneurship are expected from associations here.

111249.explanation: Monuments are our pride! First try to find money elsewhere.

111252.explanation: Especially at recycling parks, this would be nice!

111253.explanation: But it may also be somewhere else in the Achterhoek region.

111259.explanation: Tailored solutions are very important here. Look at what is best for each person.

111260.explanation: Tailored solutions are very important here. For some people, exemption from charges would be a solution, but promising exemption to everyone we do not find wise.

111263.explanation: That is logical given the increase in the number of electric cars.

111264.explanation: It is safer from a traffic perspective, and the major centers already have ring roads.

111265.explanation: This is a very important aspect especially in healthcare!

111267.explanation: Housing costs may only rise with inflation.

111269.explanation: If the revenues are spent in the tourism sector, that is okay!

111271.explanation: We see Sunday as a day of rest and wish everyone their peace.

111274.explanation: We must be frugal with agricultural land. There are enough roofs and parking lots where this can still be done.

111276.explanation: Go for it, we are proud of our Achterhoek culture.

111277.explanation: It mainly causes nuisance. We should also promote the health of our residents.

111282.explanation: This can sometimes be necessary or useful. Think, for example, of caregiver (mantelzorg) housing.

111284.explanation: We only support this if all conditions, such as noise standards and community participation, are met!

111229.explanation: This way, policies are made on issues supported by the majority of residents.

111233.explanation: This is a core task of the municipality. Besides, it is better that current energy sources like gas remain. These deserve priority.

111234.explanation: Distribution centers are of economic importance.

111235.explanation: Asylum seeker centers (AZCs) must remain banned from Bronckhorst. The municipality is not suitable for them. AZCs foster unrest and aggression and destabilize the community and living comfort.

111236.explanation: Definitely. The other housing is intended for the municipality's residents.

111238.explanation: They cause a lot of health nuisance and air pollution.

111244.explanation: The social assistance benefit is sufficient to finance this.

111245.explanation: Local broadcasters only spread left-wing news, so no extra funding.

111248.explanation: Sport is the foundation of physical and mental health.

111249.explanation: Costs are too high and money can be better spent on other matters.

111252.explanation: Cameras violate privacy. The police and public order officers should take over this work and punish strictly.

111253.explanation: These houses are sufficiently available in other cities.

111259.explanation: Mandatory volunteer work should be imposed. If not, cut benefits or eventually stop them.

111260.explanation: Everyone has to pay taxes, including low-income earners.

111263.explanation: These are already sufficiently present. The trend is shifting back to petrol and diesel cars.

111264.explanation: The maximum speed should be set at 50 km/h.

111265.explanation: Healthcare must be independent of this.

111267.explanation: The property tax (OZB) must be lowered and eventually abolished.

111269.explanation: This form of taxation should be reduced and phased out eventually.

111271.explanation: People should be allowed to shop on Sundays if they wish.

111274.explanation: Agricultural land should be used as such and not for other purposes.

111276.explanation: Traditions must be kept alive to strengthen identity.

111277.explanation: Coffeeshops should be banned. They cause nuisance and harm health.

111282.explanation: Temporary housing costs too much and is unnecessary.

111284.explanation: Wind turbines are landscape pollution and bad for health.

111285.explanation: A fine way to create additional economic activity for farmers.

111229.explanation: We want to develop a participation policy so residents can participate in all areas. A simple yes/no or for/against stance is undesirable. Issues are often too complex for that.

111233.explanation: Projects and initiatives started by residents deserve support from the municipality.

111234.explanation: It's not fitting to build large block buildings here; locations along highways are much better for that.

111235.explanation: We must comply with the dispersal law. With the designated locations, we comply, so no new shelter location will be added.

111236.explanation: If they bring in seasonal workers from abroad to perform seasonal work here, they must also ensure housing as good hosts.

111238.explanation: In many cases, a fireplace or wood stove is used to (additionally) heat the home. In certain weather conditions like dense fog, it is discouraged, but a ban goes too far.

111244.explanation: Sports are good for health and important for prevention. Membership in a club allows residents to be part of society.

111245.explanation: A local broadcaster contributes to a healthy democracy.

111248.explanation: Sport is essential; we must ensure clubs can survive. This sometimes means we must contribute extra.

111249.explanation: We have many monuments, attracting many tourists. Maintenance is becoming more expensive; cutting budgets could lead to neglect.

111252.explanation: Camera surveillance should only be used when necessary, such as in busy nightlife venues. We don't have those, so extra camera surveillance is not needed.

111253.explanation: We want good cooperation with neighboring municipalities. It doesn't have to be in Bronckhorst.

111259.explanation: If possible, everyone should do socially meaningful work. There can be good reasons why this is not possible, such as a disability or illness, in which case we should not require it from them.

111260.explanation: It is part of our poverty policy; waivers can contribute to a dignified life.

111263.explanation: There are still relatively few places where you can charge an electric car. We need to make up ground on that.

111264.explanation: Major through roads should remain 50 km/h where the situation allows. In residential neighborhoods and very busy streets in centers, safety is paramount and 30 km/h is the norm to improve it.

111265.explanation: Quality of care is paramount. Additional requirements regarding religion and background can jeopardize that guarantee.

111267.explanation: Bronckhorst has relatively high housing costs, and we do not want to increase them further. We want to keep the burden on residents as equal as possible. If the property tax (OZB) must increase, other levies must be reduced, such as waste disposal fees.

111269.explanation: The tourist tax is currently very low and is entirely spent on tourism. Tourists spend money here which stimulates the local economy; more tourist tax can be charged for that.

111271.explanation: It is already allowed now, and we want to keep it that way. Entrepreneurs should be able to decide for themselves whether to open on Sundays.

111274.explanation: Preemptively banning is unrealistic. If agricultural land is not profitable or is no longer used because farmers stop, we should be able to place solar panels there.

111276.explanation: We do not decide on the content of education. However, we do want to promote the Achterhoeks dialect.

111277.explanation: The use of drugs should not be encouraged.

111282.explanation: To solve the housing shortage, we like to think outside the box. This includes temporary and flexible housing forms. Everyone should be able to find suitable accommodation.

111284.explanation: Only if all conditions are met, such as the minimum distance to homes, which is currently unclear.

111285.explanation: This is a good way to help our farmers solve the manure problem.

statements.title: Bronckhorst should allow the construction of temporary housing.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: Residents of Bronckhorst should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Bronckhorst moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted in Bronckhorst.

titleParts.text: In Bronckhorst moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: See distribution center.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Bronckhorst should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to give attention to the Achterhoeks dialect.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Bronckhorst should have a women's shelter.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality must prevent the opening of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The property tax (ozb) should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should allow farmers to build manure digesters so they can extract gas from their animals' manure.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet boeren toestaan een

titleParts.glossary: Installation that produces gas from livestock manure, usually on the farm itself.

titleParts.text: te bouwen, waarmee ze gas kunnen halen uit de poep van hun vee.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Bronckhorst should allow large wind turbines.

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.
